description:
You are acting as a Kubernetes platform-operator who needs a quick textual overview of the
    Deployment manifests that live in the directory
    “/home/user/manifests”.  
    Three JSON‐formatted manifests are **already present** in that directory:

    1. /home/user/manifests/frontend-deploy.json  
    2. /home/user/manifests/backend-deploy.json  
    3. /home/user/manifests/worker-deploy.json  

    Your job is to extract a few specific fields from every **object whose
    "kind" equals "Deployment"** and to create two new artefacts that a CI
    pipeline will later consume:

    A) /home/user/manifest-summary.csv  
       • This file must contain exactly **five comma-separated columns in
         this order**:  
           namespace,name,image,replicas,kind  
       • The first line must be the header shown above (lower-case).  
       • One subsequent line per Deployment.  
       • Lines must be sorted **alphabetically first by “namespace”, then by
         “name”**.  
       • There must be **no extra spaces** anywhere in the file.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. “\n”).  
       Example of the required layout (values will match the actual
       manifests):  
       ```
       namespace,name,image,replicas,kind
       batch,worker,company/worker:v5,4,Deployment
       prod,backend,company/backend:v1,2,Deployment
       prod,frontend,company/frontend:v2,3,Deployment
       ```

    B) /home/user/namespace-counts.json  
       • A JSON object where every key is a namespace discovered among the
         Deployments and every value is the integer count of Deployments in
         that namespace.  
       • Keys must be in **alphabetical order**.  
       • The file must be pretty-printed with **two-space indentation** and
         must terminate with a single trailing newline.  
       • Example of the exact formatting required:  
       ```
       {
         "batch": 1,
         "prod": 2
       }
       ```

    Use whatever standard shell utilities (jq, awk, sort, etc.) you prefer,
    but keep it to a simple sequence of roughly three to four commands.
    When you are finished, the two artefacts must exist at the exact paths
    and in the exact formats described above so that an automated test can
    validate them.
